gaps: Final pay POST is not replayable from a static Go binary (CSRF + 3DS + likely CAPTCHA). This CLI does not attempt booking; it stays read-only against the search/results, shoulder-dates, airports, and atmos-rewards endpoints.
Seat-map view of a specific reservation not captured this run. Reservation lookup (existing PNR) also not captured.

Family-of-N seat-together filtering, fare-drop watcher, multi-city composer, award-vs-cash comparator, and pre-checkout deeplink builder were scoped in the manuscript but not implemented in this generation; the CLI ships without them.

novel_features.name: Field selection on deep JSON
novel_features.description: Generic --select flag narrows the ~50KB search/results JSON to just the fields the agent needs.
novel_features.rationale: Reading 50KB into agent context per search is expensive; --select cuts to 1-2KB.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets the agent ask for exactly what it needs and skip the chrome.

novel_features_built.name: Field selection on deep JSON
novel_features_built.description: Generic --select flag narrows the ~50KB search/results JSON to just the fields the agent needs.
novel_features_built.rationale: Reading 50KB into agent context per search is expensive; --select cuts to 1-2KB.
novel_features_built.why_it_matters: Lets the agent ask for exactly what it needs and skip the chrome.

narrative.headline: Search Alaska Airlines flights and check Atmos Rewards balance from the terminal, with offline-cached airports and agent-native JSON output.
narrative.value_prop: Single static binary that talks to alaskaair.com's SvelteKit endpoints over a Chrome-fingerprinted HTTP transport. Offline SQLite cache for the airport catalog. Cookie-import auth via your logged-in Chrome session for endpoints that need a guestsession JWT.
narrative.auth_narrative: Run `auth login --chrome` once. It extracts Alaska's cookies from your logged-in Chrome profile (AS_ACNT, AS_NAME, guestsession, ASSession, etc.) via your macOS keychain. Future commands replay them via Surf transport with a Chrome TLS fingerprint. Run `doctor` to verify the session is valid.

quickstart.comment: import your Alaska cookies from Chrome - one-time setup
search for a family of 6, round trip
check current Atmos Rewards points balance

troubleshoots.symptom: auth login fails with keychain access denied
troubleshoots.fix: macOS asked Chrome for 'Chrome Safe Storage' access; click Always Allow in the keychain prompt and retry

troubleshoots.symptom: search returns empty results but the website works
troubleshoots.fix: your guestsession JWT may be expired - run `auth login --chrome` to re-import cookies
the Atmos Rewards token cookie expired; run `auth login --chrome` to re-import cookies
rebuild the binary or set ALASKA_DISABLE_SURF=1 to fall back to stdlib http (lower-fidelity fingerprint, may be flagged)

narrative.when_to_use: Pick this CLI when an agent needs to search Alaska flights or check Atmos Rewards data programmatically with cached airport lookup and trimmed JSON output. Don't pick it for booking automation; the final pay POST is not replayable from a static binary.

recipes.title: Trim a flights search to just price and flight number
recipes.explanation: Cuts the ~50KB results payload to just flight number, duration, and saver fare. Avoids dumping the full payload into agent context.

recipes.title: Family search round trip
recipes.explanation: Round-trip fare matrix for two adults and four children across all cabin classes.

recipes.title: Flexible-date pricing matrix
recipes.explanation: Get fares for dates near your target departure so you can spot a cheaper shoulder date without N separate searches.

recipes.title: Atmos Rewards balance
recipes.explanation: Current Atmos Rewards points balance for the cookie-session account.

recipes.title: Airport lookup
recipes.explanation: Pull airport details (city, region, lat/lon, codeshare carriers) for a single IATA code from the local store.
